projects.title: Robot search and evacuation problem
projects.description: Ongoing project. In collaboration with a postgraduate student, under the supervision of my professor. Studying upper and lower bounds for the search and evacuation problem of robots on a unit disk. Gained foundations in computational geometry and learned how to apply numerical methods to derive upper bounds.

projects.title: Randomized Decision Tree Complexity for Recursive Majority Decision Trees
projects.description: Ongoing project. Supervised by Prof. Nikos Leonardos. Developing a randomized algorithm to evaluate a recursive majority decision tree. Studied the state of the art in recursive majority decision tree complexity, online learning, and basic information theory. Currently working on implementing potential functions to track algorithm progress.

projects.title: Personal Website
projects.description: Finished but regularly updated. A modern academic portfolio showcasing my background, teaching materials, and research projects.

projects.title: Woundy: 3D Wound Scanning & Assessment Device
projects.description: Ongoing project. As part of a student team, developing a biomedical device for automated 3D wound imaging and volumetric reconstruction. Designed algorithms for sensor data denoising and 3D surface reconstruction to enable automated clinical assessment. Leading the software implementation and integration of the imaging prototype.

projects.title: Lower Bounds for Randomized Decision Tree Complexity for k-Threshold Gate Trees
projects.description: Past project. Supervised by Prof. Nikos Leonardos. Developed a recursive formula for calculating lower bounds on the randomized decision tree complexity of decision trees with k-threshold gates. Derived closed-form expressions for two specific subcases. Gained understanding of decision tree complexity (classical, randomized, and quantum) and studied how recursive formulas can be expanded into closed forms.
